content: We can refactor curiosity-driven reinforcement learning (CDRL) agents to use a two-module intrinsic reward system modeled directly on prefrontal-hippocampal interaction during memory retrieval: the prefrontal cortex-inspired ICM/RND module evaluates prediction error to guide curiosity, while the hippocampus-inspired episodic memory module gates which novel state prediction errors are actually consolidated to drive future curiosity. This neurobiologically-grounded gating solves the longstanding CDRL problem of wasted intrinsic reward on noisy, unlearnable distracting states.
source_topics: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
trigger_topic: curiosity driven reinforcement learning ICM RND compared to curious agent
